commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a macOS interactive CLI hook to offer the existing legacy Codex OAuth sidecar migration, adds a headless Keychain warning, updates doctor wording/docs/changelog, and expands regression tests.

PR surface: Source +196, Tests +653, Docs +1. Total +850 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: on macOS with an interactive TTY and a migratable legacy sidecar, `openclaw channels remove --channel <name> --delete` reaches the new auto-migrate prompt because the hook only skips JSON/global no-prompt flags. I did not run the flow because this review is read-only and the bug is clear from the command contract plus hook code.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can add the missing command-scoped no-prompt skip and tests; broader auth/upgrade policy review remains maintainer-owned after that blocker is removed.

target.title: fix(auth): auto-migrate keychain-only legacy Codex OAuth profiles on first interactive CLI run
target.body:
## Summary

Closes the remaining bucket from #85083: macOS users whose legacy `oauthRef` sidecar seed lives only in the Keychain still had to know to run `openclaw doctor --fix` after upgrade, because the embedded-runtime self-heal that landed in #85074 intentionally runs with `allowKeychainPrompt: false`.

The fix adds a tiny pre-command trigger in `runCli` (`src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts`) that, on darwin + TTY only, reuses the existing `maybeRepairLegacyOAuthSidecarProfiles` doctor flow when a sidecar dir is present. Fast bail-outs make the cost a single `readdirSync` for users without legacy sidecars. Declining writes a permanent decline marker in the state dir (`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/legacy-oauth-sidecar-migration-declined`) so we never pester the user again; running `openclaw doctor --fix` is the documented retry path. `OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR=0` is an explicit opt-out for power users.

Headless paths (cron, systemd, Telegram polling, embedded sub-agent dispatch) still skip the prompt — but the legacy sidecar loader now emits a one-shot `log.warn` that names `openclaw doctor --fix` and macOS Keychain, instead of letting the credential silently fall through to a downstream `No API key found for provider "openai-codex"`.

Also drops the user-confusing `sidecar-backed` phrasing from the doctor prompt/notes/changes; existing internal identifier names are left intact.

## Approach

Approach **B** from the issue (auto-trigger doctor migration on first interactive `openclaw <anything>` invocation). Architecturally cleaner than threading TTY-awareness into the embedded runtime: the trigger reuses doctor's existing migrate function as-is, and "when is it safe to prompt for Keychain access?" stays one decision, one place.

Skip rules in order: `process.platform !== "darwin"` → `!isInteractiveTty` → `CI=true || OPENCLAW_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 || OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR=0` → `OPENCLAW_AUTH_STORE_READONLY=1` → `--help/--version` → primary is `doctor`/`update`/`help`/`completion`/`version` → `--json`/`--json=…` (anywhere in argv before `--`) → global `NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` (`--non-interactive`/`--yes`/`--no-input`/`--force`) → command-scoped no-prompt opts in `COMMAND_PATH_NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` (currently `channels remove --delete`, which is documented as no-prompt) → sidecar dir empty → fresh decline marker. All flag checks honor the `--` argv terminator.

The platform check is hoisted to the call site in `runCli` so non-darwin pays zero dynamic-import cost.

## Headless one-shot warning

Added in `legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts`. Fires when `loadLegacyOAuthSidecarMaterial` returns null on darwin with `allowKeychainPrompt: false` (i.e., env/file seeds were tried and Keychain was blocked). Rate-limited to one warn per process so embedded turns log it once per gateway lifetime, not per attempt. Guarded by the same VITEST env signals as the keychain read so unrelated test runs don't surface it.

## Regression tests

- `src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts` — table-driven preflight skip checks for each rule (including the new command-scoped `channels remove --delete` skip with positive cases, anti-regression for `channels remove` without `--delete`, `--delete` outside the `channels remove` path, and `--delete` after a `--` terminator), plus end-to-end migrate / decline / permanent skip using `createOpenClawTestState`.
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts` — exercises the new headless warn path: emit once when blocked on darwin with no env/file seeds; never emit on non-darwin.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/commands/agents.add.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts` — 80/80 green.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` clean on all touched files; `oxfmt --check` clean.
- Wider `src/agents/auth-profiles` shard has the same 5 file / 26 test pre-existing flakes vs `origin/main` (`--isolate=false` cross-file mock leakage in unrelated OAuth tests). My new tests do not contribute to those failures; if anything, isolating mine reduces the count.

## Scope notes

- Did not touch `agents.add.test.ts` / `stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts` test descriptions that still contain `sidecar-backed`; those are dev-facing and reference implementation, not UI copy.
- Did not externalize `OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR` to docs anywhere except `docs/gateway/doctor.md` accordion 5 since it is an escape hatch, not a normal-user knob.
- No changes to `maybeRepairLegacyOAuthSidecarProfiles` itself beyond the wording rename — the migration semantics are intentionally identical between the doctor-driven and CLI-startup-driven paths.
- `COMMAND_PATH_NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` is a separate data-driven list from `NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` deliberately: `--delete` only means "no prompt" on `channels remove`, so it must not be promoted to the global skip set where it would over-match unrelated commands.

## Out of scope

- Removing the legacy sidecar code paths entirely. Tracked separately and should wait for the long tail to migrate.
- Changes to `openclaw doctor --fix` behavior itself; the migration path it runs is already correct.

## Related

- Closes #85083
- Companion to #85074 (file/env-seed bucket) and #85028 (fail-fast on missing refresh token)
- Original surface: #84893 / #84752 / #83312
target.author: romneyda

commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission none are not allowed

- `src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts` — exercises the new headless warn path: emit once when blocked on darwin with no env/file seeds; never emit on non-darwin.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/commands/agents.add.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts` — 80/80 green.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` clean on all touched files; `oxfmt --check` clean.
- Wider `src/agents/auth-profiles` shard has the same 5 file / 26 test pre-existing flakes vs `origin/main` (`--isolate=false` cross-file mock leakage in unrelated OAuth tests). My new tests do not contribute to those failures; if anything, isolating mine reduces the count.

## Scope notes

- Did not touch `agents.add.test.ts` / `stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts` test descriptions that still contain `sidecar-backed`; those are dev-facing and reference implementation, not UI copy.
- Did not externalize `OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR` to docs anywhere except `docs/gateway/doctor.md` accordion 5 since it is an escape hatch, not a normal-user knob.
- No changes to `maybeRepairLegacyOAuthSidecarProfiles` itself beyond the wording rename — the migration semantics are intentionally identical between the doctor-driven and CLI-startup-driven paths.
- `COMMAND_PATH_NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` is a separate data-driven list from `NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` deliberately: `--delete` only means "no prompt" on `channels remove`, so it must not be promoted to the global skip set where it would over-match unrelated commands.

## Out of scope

- Removing the legacy sidecar code paths entirely. Tracked separately and should wait for the long tail to migrate.
- Changes to `openclaw doctor --fix` behavior itself; the migration path it runs is already correct.

## Related

- Closes #85083
- Companion to #85074 (file/env-seed bucket) and #85028 (fail-fast on missing refresh token)
- Original surface: #84893 / #84752 / #83312

commands.author: RomneyDa

target.title: fix(auth): auto-migrate keychain-only legacy Codex OAuth profiles on first interactive CLI run
target.body:
## Summary

Closes the remaining bucket from #85083: macOS users whose legacy `oauthRef` sidecar seed lives only in the Keychain still had to know to run `openclaw doctor --fix` after upgrade, because the embedded-runtime self-heal that landed in #85074 intentionally runs with `allowKeychainPrompt: false`.

The fix adds a tiny pre-command trigger in `runCli` (`src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts`) that, on darwin + TTY only, reuses the existing `maybeRepairLegacyOAuthSidecarProfiles` doctor flow when a sidecar dir is present. Fast bail-outs make the cost a single `readdirSync` for users without legacy sidecars. Declining writes a permanent decline marker in the state dir (`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/legacy-oauth-sidecar-migration-declined`) so we never pester the user again; running `openclaw doctor --fix` is the documented retry path. `OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR=0` is an explicit opt-out for power users.

Headless paths (cron, systemd, Telegram polling, embedded sub-agent dispatch) still skip the prompt — but the legacy sidecar loader now emits a one-shot `log.warn` that names `openclaw doctor --fix` and macOS Keychain, instead of letting the credential silently fall through to a downstream `No API key found for provider "openai-codex"`.

Also drops the user-confusing `sidecar-backed` phrasing from the doctor prompt/notes/changes; existing internal identifier names are left intact.

## Approach

Approach **B** from the issue (auto-trigger doctor migration on first interactive `openclaw <anything>` invocation). Architecturally cleaner than threading TTY-awareness into the embedded runtime: the trigger reuses doctor's existing migrate function as-is, and "when is it safe to prompt for Keychain access?" stays one decision, one place.

Skip rules in order: `process.platform !== "darwin"` → `!isInteractiveTty` → `CI=true || OPENCLAW_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 || OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR=0` → `OPENCLAW_AUTH_STORE_READONLY=1` → `--help/--version` → primary is `doctor`/`update`/`help`/`completion`/`version` → `--json`/`--json=…` (anywhere in argv before `--`) → global `NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` (`--non-interactive`/`--yes`/`--no-input`/`--force`) → command-scoped no-prompt opts in `COMMAND_PATH_NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` (currently `channels remove --delete`, which is documented as no-prompt) → sidecar dir empty → fresh decline marker. All flag checks honor the `--` argv terminator.

The platform check is hoisted to the call site in `runCli` so non-darwin pays zero dynamic-import cost.

## Headless one-shot warning

Added in `legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts`. Fires when `loadLegacyOAuthSidecarMaterial` returns null on darwin with `allowKeychainPrompt: false` (i.e., env/file seeds were tried and Keychain was blocked). Rate-limited to one warn per process so embedded turns log it once per gateway lifetime, not per attempt. Guarded by the same VITEST env signals as the keychain read so unrelated test runs don't surface it.

## Regression tests

- `src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts` — table-driven preflight skip checks for each rule (including the new command-scoped `channels remove --delete` skip with positive cases, anti-regression for `channels remove` without `--delete`, `--delete` outside the `channels remove` path, and `--delete` after a `--` terminator), plus end-to-end migrate / decline / permanent skip using `createOpenClawTestState`.
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts` — exercises the new headless warn path: emit once when blocked on darwin with no env/file seeds; never emit on non-darwin.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/auto-migrate-legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/commands/agents.add.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts` — 80/80 green.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` clean on all touched files; `oxfmt --check` clean.
- Wider `src/agents/auth-profiles` shard has the same 5 file / 26 test pre-existing flakes vs `origin/main` (`--isolate=false` cross-file mock leakage in unrelated OAuth tests). My new tests do not contribute to those failures; if anything, isolating mine reduces the count.

## Scope notes

- Did not touch `agents.add.test.ts` / `stale-oauth-profile-shadows.test.ts` test descriptions that still contain `sidecar-backed`; those are dev-facing and reference implementation, not UI copy.
- Did not externalize `OPENCLAW_AUTO_MIGRATE_LEGACY_OAUTH_SIDECAR` to docs anywhere except `docs/gateway/doctor.md` accordion 5 since it is an escape hatch, not a normal-user knob.
- No changes to `maybeRepairLegacyOAuthSidecarProfiles` itself beyond the wording rename — the migration semantics are intentionally identical between the doctor-driven and CLI-startup-driven paths.
- `COMMAND_PATH_NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` is a separate data-driven list from `NO_PROMPT_FLAGS` deliberately: `--delete` only means "no prompt" on `channels remove`, so it must not be promoted to the global skip set where it would over-match unrelated commands.

## Out of scope

- Removing the legacy sidecar code paths entirely. Tracked separately and should wait for the long tail to migrate.
- Changes to `openclaw doctor --fix` behavior itself; the migration path it runs is already correct.

## Related

- Closes #85083
- Companion to #85074 (file/env-seed bucket) and #85028 (fail-fast on missing refresh token)
- Original surface: #84893 / #84752 / #83312
